page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-56 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 56 dedicated vCPUs derived from 28 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The memory configuration provides 448.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations place this instance in the top tier for PassMark memory performance, alongside strong results in multi-core CPU execution, software compilation with Clang, and ray tracing. Single-core performance, static web serving throughput, and large-block memory bandwidth read and write operations fall within the average range. Cryptographic and vector processing capabilities are supported by hardware flags such as AVX, AES, and SHA_NI, though AVX-512 extensions are absent.

The qualitative cost efficiency of the c2d-highmem-56 lies in its high memory density, allowing memory-intensive applications to run efficiently without the overhead of GPU hardware. However, the lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs. This server is suited for memory-bound workloads, database operations, software compilation, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-56 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server configured with 56 dedicated vCPUs and 448.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. In benchmark testing, the instance achieves top-tier memory performance and strong multi-core processing, software compilation, and ray tracing capabilities, while single-core execution and large-block memory bandwidth remain average. The absence of local NVMe storage represents a key architectural tradeoff, requiring network-attached storage solutions. This configuration is suited for database operations, memory-intensive enterprise applications, software development pipelines, and medium-scale language model inference.

tagline: A memory-dense x86_64 server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing for memory-intensive database and multi-threaded compilation workloads.
